app.tagline: Japanese the way it's spoken — 55% polite, 45% colloquial

home.freePace: Progress is free — this is just the suggested minimum to finish in 12 weeks.
home.behind: No rush — the pace is yours. 🍵

curriculum.title: 12-week path
curriculum.subtitle: Everything is unlocked from day one. The path marks the suggested pace, not a lock.
curriculum.comingSoon: This week's content is in production — the engine is ready for it.

sentence.tapHint: Tap the tiles in the right order
sentence.wrong: Almost! The order isn't right. Try again.
sentence.emptySub: This content is on the way.

flash.dailyDoneSub: That's the suggested pace so memory sticks without overload. But progress is free — you can keep going.
flash.allLearned: You've seen every hiragana! Come back later for reviews, or practice now.

trace.title: Writing practice
trace.instructions: Trace the character over the model. Mind the stroke order!
trace.attribution: Strokes: KanjiVG © Ulrich Apel, CC BY-SA 3.0

dialogue.title: Chats
dialogue.subtitle: Talk with your Japanese friends — each one lives in a different register. Chats follow your level.

onboarding.title: Welcome to KawaiiLearn!
onboarding.subtitle: Two quick questions to make the app yours.
onboarding.genderQ: How should the app refer to you?
onboarding.pronounNote: In Japanese, 'I' changes with the speaker: 私 (watashi) is universal, 僕 (boku) is casual masculine, あたし (atashi) is casual feminine. Your pick becomes a hint in lessons!
onboarding.crush_haruto: The cute barista guy who memorized your order
onboarding.crush_mei: The cute barista girl who memorized your order

yuki.bio: 22, infinite energy, speaks 100% casual and loves correcting you while laughing.

haruto.bio: Works at the café by the station. Started polite, but he's getting casual with you…

mei.bio: Works at the café by the station. Started polite, but she's getting casual with you…

kenji.bio: Always courteous, always in です・ます. Perfect for training the polite register.

hana.bio: An obaachan who gives you tangerines and speaks warm, unhurried polite Japanese.

aiko.bio: Explains everything patiently and shows you when to use each register.
